title: useToggle Hook
description:
Implement a **useToggle** custom hook that manages a boolean state.

### Requirements

- `useToggle(initialValue?)` returns an array: `[value, toggle, setValue]`
- `value` is the current boolean (default `false`)
- `toggle()` flips the value
- `setValue(bool)` sets to a specific value
- The hook must accept an optional `initialValue` parameter

### Usage Example

```tsx
const [isOn, toggle, setIsOn] = useToggle(false);
toggle();     // isOn → true
toggle();     // isOn → false
setIsOn(true); // isOn → true
```

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Display value | `toggle-value` |
| Toggle button | `toggle-btn` |
| Set True button | `set-true` |
| Set False button | `set-false` |
constraints:
- Implement as a standalone custom hook in useToggle.ts
- Use only React built-in hooks (useState, useCallback)
- The hook must be reusable across components
hints: Use useState<boolean> to store the value.
The toggle function should use the callback form of setState: prev => !prev.
Wrap toggle and setValue in useCallback for referential stability.
